title: Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
description:
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen?
This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.

allStepDetails.content:
Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Problem Statement and Objective
Detailed Instructions:
 Problem Statement 
Telecom revenue performance varies significantly across regions and customer segments, but business teams lack a clear, consolidated view to understand where growth is happening and where revenue is at risk. While quarterly revenue is increasing, fluctuations in churn and subscriber behavior create uncertainty in forecasting and budget planning. Without a unified view of revenue, subscribers, churn, and network performance over time, finance and strategy teams struggle to make confident, data-driven decisions. This project addresses that gap by transforming raw telecom data into a business-ready dashboard that enables accurate performance monitoring and forward-looking planning. 

Objective
The objective of this project is to create a unified, analytics-ready telecom dataset that enables accurate revenue forecasting and KPI analysis across regions and customer segments. This solution supports finance and strategy teams by improving visibility into revenue drivers, churn risk, and customer behavior to enable better planning and decision-making.
Insight: 

How to prepare a problem statement

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Executive Summary and Background
Detailed Instructions:

Executive Summary
This project focuses on transforming, enriching, and analyzing Vodafone’s telecom performance data to uncover insights that support smarter decisions across the business. Using Numtra, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, the dataset was cleaned, engineered, and optimized for advanced analytics and machine-learning workflows. The final unified dataset combines business-friendly information with ML-ready features, enabling accurate forecasting, performance measurement, and strategic planning. By leveraging these tools, the project empowers teams to understand customer trends, revenue patterns, and operational efficiency with far greater clarity.

 Background
The Vodafone Qatar dataset covers fiscal years 2021–2023, capturing key operational and financial KPIs from official investor presentations. It includes metrics such as revenue, subscribers, ARPU, data usage, churn, customer satisfaction, and digital adoption rates. The dataset highlights Vodafone Qatar’s growth in revenue, 5G network expansion, and increased digital sales share, offering a foundation for time-series forecasting and business performance analysis.

Insight: 

How to write an Executive Summary

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Data Overview
Detailed Instructions:
Dataset Link:  https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/finance123456/vodafone
The Vodafone Qatar dataset was obtained from the Kaggle open data platform.
It was curated using structured performance data extracted from Vodafone Qatar’s official investor presentations covering fiscal years 2021 to 2023.
The dataset includes key performance indicators such as:
Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) 
Customer satisfaction
Digital sales penetration
Churn rate 
Revenue and net profit 
Complaint resolution metrics 
Network expansion data, including 5G rollout progress
The data reflects Vodafone Qatar’s strategic shift toward digital platforms, operational efficiency, and enhanced customer experience.
It enables comprehensive analysis of:
Financial performance 
Subscriber behavior 
Digital transformation trends 
Broader telecom market insights within the MENA region

System Architecture Overview
Raw telecom data was ingested and cleaned using Numtra and Python to ensure consistency and analytical readiness. The processed data was then stored, modeled, and managed within Microsoft Fabric as a centralized data layer. This enriched dataset powered KPI calculations, interactive dashboards, and forecasting-ready features in Power BI, enabling business users to explore trends, monitor performance, and support data-driven strategic decisions.

Data Features 

The dataset includes a comprehensive set of features designed to analyze telecom performance across time, customer segments, and regions. Core business metrics such as Revenue, Total Subscribers, Average ARPU, Churn Rate, and Network Availability provide a holistic view of financial health, customer behavior, and service quality. 

Time-based attributes including Year, Quarter, and Date enable trend analysis, period-over-period comparisons, and seasonal insights. Regional and segment-level fields allow performance to be evaluated by geography and customer type, supporting targeted analysis and strategic planning. 

To enhance analytical and forecasting capabilities, several calculated features were created, such as Revenue QoQ (%), Subscriber QoQ (%), ARPU QoQ (%), and QoQ Churn Change (%), enabling faster identification of growth patterns and risk areas. These enriched features make the dataset both business-friendly for dashboard users and ML-ready for revenue forecasting, supporting data-driven decisions across finance and strategy teams. 

Insight: 

Datasets

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Methodology
Detailed Instructions:

  Data Preparation
Raw telecom data from Vodafone Qatar was first cleaned and standardized to ensure consistency and accuracy. Missing values, formatting issues, and inconsistent metrics were resolved. A centralized Date table was created to enable reliable time-based analysis across all KPIs. Key metrics such as revenue, subscribers, churn, ARPU, and network availability were validated and prepared for analysis.

Model Selection 
To support quarterly revenue forecasting, supervised regression techniques were selected due to their ability to learn from historical trends and structured business data. The modeling process was performed using Nexus AutoML, which automatically evaluates multiple regression algorithms and compares their performance. 
Nexus tested over 30 candidate models and identified LassoLarsCV as the best-performing model based on cross-validation results. This approach ensured an objective and data-driven selection of the most suitable forecasting model without manual bias. 
Model Development
The revenue forecasting model was developed using Nexus AutoML in a development environment. Historical telecom data was split into training and validation sets, with 240 records used for training and 60 records for cross-validation. 

Nexus automated feature handling, hyperparameter tuning, and model comparison, allowing the model to be trained efficiently while maintaining consistency and reproducibility. This streamlined development process reduced manual effort and ensured reliable model training. 
Model Performance  
Model performance was evaluated using cross-validation metrics provided by Nexus AutoML. The selected model achieved an R² score of 0.90, indicating a strong ability to explain revenue variance. 

Error metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (27.4) and Mean Squared Error (1003.7) demonstrate that the model produces stable and reliable quarterly revenue predictions. These results confirm that the model is suitable for strategic forecasting and financial planning use cases.

Insight: 

Data Preparation for ML development

Step Name:Visualization
Detailed Instructions:
The Vodafone Telecom Performance Dashboard provides a comprehensive view of business performance across revenue, subscribers, churn, and network reliability from 2021 to 2023. It visualizes key KPIs such as Total Revenue, Revenue QoQ growth, Total Subscribers, Churn Rate, Average ARPU, and Network Availability to help stakeholders quickly assess overall health. 

Time-based trends highlight how revenue and subscriber growth evolve quarter over quarter, while regional and segment-level visuals reveal performance differences across customer types. Churn and retention visuals expose risk areas requiring intervention, while high network availability reinforces service stability. Together, these visualizations enable finance and strategy teams to monitor performance, identify risks, and support data-driven planning and forecasting decisions. 

Insight: 

Power BI Dashboard Tutorial

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Case 1
Detailed Instructions:
Case 1 - Increase Revenue Forecast Accuracy to Improve Budget Allocation

Objective: Improve quarterly revenue forecasting to optimize investment across high-growth segments.
 
Positive Insight
Total revenue increased 14% QoQ, reaching $108K, with Doha contributing the largest share supported by its 72M subscribers. This strong momentum indicates predictable quarterly patterns that can be further optimized with more accurate forecasting. 
Negative Insight
Churn for high-value mobile prepaid customers remains above 2.5%, increasing by 0.3% QoQ, which reduces revenue stability and increases forecasting uncertainty.
ROI (Revenue Gain Potential)
Forecasting improvement of 10% typically reduces budget overspend by 3–5%.
• Wasted budget ˜ 5% of revenue = $5,400/year
• ML forecasting implementation = $1,200/year 

Estimated Savings: $5,400 – $1,200 = $4,200
ROI: (4,200 / 1,200)  100 = 350%
Insight:

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Case 2
Detailed Instructions:

Case 2 — Reduce Segment Churn to Increase Retained Revenue

Objective: Lower churn in key telecom segments to protect recurring revenue.

Positive Insight

The network availability score is extremely high at 99.95%, supporting strong customer experience and allowing retention efforts to work with a reliable service foundation.

Negative Insight

Churn in segments like Mobile Postpaid and Fixed Broadband ranges 2.41%–2.74%, higher than the overall churn rate of 2.51%, creating an avoidable annual revenue loss estimated at $8–12K.

ROI (Churn Reduction Savings)

Reducing churn by even 0.5% in high-value segments is achievable through micro-targeted retention campaigns.

Calculation:
• Revenue per customer ˜ $110 ARPU
• Estimated at-risk customers (0.5% of 72M) ˜ 360K
• Prevented churn revenue impact (industry average 60% recoverable) ? $2.4M recoverable
• Program cost: $40,000/year

Estimated Revenue Protected: $2,400,000
ROI: = (2,400,000 – 40,000) / 40,000  100 = 5,900%
 
 Chart for Case 1 and Case 2 comparison:

Summary

Both cases offer strong business value. Case 1 improves financial planning by increasing forecast accuracy, generating moderate savings and a solid 350% ROI. Case 2 provides significantly higher impact by reducing churn, protecting major recurring revenue, and achieving a 5,900% ROI. The choice depends on whether stability or growth protection is the priority.

Insight:

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Solution Impact
Detailed Instructions:

 Solution Impact
This solution transforms raw telecom data into a unified analytics and forecasting platform that enables proactive decision-making. By combining historical performance, customer behavior, and operational KPIs into a single dashboard and ML-ready dataset, the project reduces uncertainty in revenue planning and highlights risk areas such as churn and segment volatility. Finance and strategy teams gain faster access to insights that previously required manual analysis.

 Business Outcomes 
Improved visibility into quarterly revenue trends by region and segment, supporting more accurate budgeting and investment decisions
Early identification of high-churn segments, enabling targeted retention strategies before revenue loss occurs
Clear alignment between network performance, customer behavior, and financial outcomes, strengthening cross-team collaboration
Reduced reliance on static reports, enabling self-service analytics for business stakeholders

 Key Achievements 
Built an end-to-end analytics pipeline from data ingestion and transformation to dashboarding and forecasting
Delivered a business-ready Power BI dashboard with KPIs, trends, and segment-level insights
Created an ML-ready dataset supporting supervised revenue forecasting models
Translated complex technical outputs into clear, executive-friendly insights
Demonstrated measurable ROI potential through forecasting accuracy and churn reduction use cases

 Challenges and Solutions

1: Date Table & Time Intelligence Issues 
Challenge
After marking the Date table as a Date Table in Power BI, the automatic date hierarchy disappeared. This caused confusion when creating Year slicers and resulted in errors while applying time-based DAX functions, delaying KPI trend analysis. 

Solution
A dedicated Year column was created using DAX and used directly in slicers. Time intelligence calculations were aligned to the marked Date table, restoring consistent QoQ and trend comparisons. 
 
2: KPI Card Formatting & Transparency Limitations 
Challenge
Some KPI/Card visuals did not support background transparency or flexible formatting, making it difficult to align KPIs with the dashboard’s background image and design theme. 

Solution
Card background effects were disabled, and transparency was controlled through value and multi-card layout settings. This ensured visual consistency without sacrificing readability. 
 
3: Network Availability KPI Misinterpretation 
Challenge
The Network Availability KPI initially displayed a value of 9995.05%, which was misleading and unrealistic for a percentage-based metric, raising concerns about calculation accuracy. 

Solution
The DAX calculation was corrected by scaling the metric correctly (dividing by 100) and applying proper percentage formatting, resulting in an accurate and interpretable KPI. 
 
4: Aligning Business KPIs with ML-Ready Features 
Challenge
Ensuring that KPIs used in dashboards aligned with features required for revenue forecasting models required careful validation across transformations and aggregations. 

Solution
KPIs were derived consistently from the enriched dataset, ensuring that dashboard metrics and machine-learning features were based on the same business logic and time granularity.
 Lessons Learned 
Strong data modeling is critical:
Creating a proper Date table early prevents downstream issues with time intelligence and KPI comparisons.
Validate KPIs before visualizing:
Always sanity-check KPI values (especially percentages) to ensure calculations and formatting align with real-world expectations.
Design choices impact insight clarity:
Visual consistency, transparency, and color usage significantly improve how easily stakeholders interpret KPIs.
Business context matters as much as accuracy:
KPIs like churn, ARPU, and revenue trends must be presented in a way that clearly supports decision-making, not just technical correctness.
Early stakeholder feedback saves time:
Iterating on visuals and messaging early helps align insights with business expectations and reduces rework later.

 Future Capability 
Enable multi-quarter and multi-year revenue forecasting by segment and region.
Integrate real-time or near–real-time data for continuous performance monitoring.
Extend predictive models to include churn risk and customer lifetime value forecasting.
Support scenario analysis for budgeting and strategic planning.
Enhance automation within Microsoft Fabric for scalable enterprise deployment.

Insight: 

How to import CSV data inside the Power BI

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Create New Date Table
Detailed Instructions:
Click on Modeling  Ribbon 
Click on New Table then put this DAX formula into it : 
DateTable = 
VAR MinDate = MINX( ALL( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset' ), 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Date] )
VAR MaxDate = MAXX( ALL( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset' ), 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Date] )
RETURN
ADDCOLUMNS(
     CALENDAR( MinDate, MaxDate ),
     "QuarterNumber", ROUNDUP(MONTH([Date])/3, 0),
     "QuarterLabel", "Q" & FORMAT( ROUNDUP(MONTH([Date])/3,0), "0" ),
     "YearQuarter", FORMAT([Date],"yyyy") & " " & "Q" & FORMAT( ROUNDUP(MONTH([Date])/3,0),"0" ),
     "QuarterStart", DATE( YEAR([Date]), (ROUNDUP(MONTH([Date])/3,0)-1)*3 + 1, 1 )
) 
Click Enter or Click the tick sign to save 
Go to the Date Table Date column and select Modeling 
Select Mark as Date Table and turn it to ON, Choose the Date column
Create new columns to hold the Year, Quarter, Month Name, Month and YearQuarter.
Go to table tools -> New column -> fill these DAX formulas one by one: 
      Year = YEAR ( 'DateTable'[Date] )
      Quarter = "Q" & FORMAT('DateTable'[Date], "Q")
      Month = MONTH('DateTable'[Date])
      Month Name = FORMAT('DateTable'[Date], "MMM")
      YearQuarter = YEAR('DateTable'[Date]) & "-Q" & FORMAT('DateTable'[Date], "Q")

Insight: 

How to Create a DATE TABLE in Power BI

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Create Measures table and Add New measures
Detailed Instructions:
Create New table called Measure Table. Then go to Modeling and Click on New Measures. Then insert the DAX formula for the Total Revenue  

Total Revenue = SUM('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Revenue_QR_Mn]) 

Then Click the tick mark to save it. By using the same step create New Measures for the following DAX formulas:
 
Total Subscribers = 
 SUM( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Subscribers] ) 
Average ARPU = 
 AVERAGE( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[ARPU_QR] ) 
Avg Revenue per Region = 
 AVERAGEX(
     VALUES('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Region]),
     CALCULATE( SUM('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Revenue_QR_Mn]) )
 ) 
Churn Rate (%) = 
 AVERAGE( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Churn_Rate_Pct] )/100 
 Customer Retention Rate % = 
 1 - [Churn Rate (%)] 
 Network Availability (%) = 
 AVERAGE( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Network_Availability_Pct] )/100 
QoQ Churn Change (%) = 
 VAR CurrentChurn =
     AVERAGE ( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Churn_Rate_Pct] )
 VAR PrevChurn =
     CALCULATE (
         AVERAGE ( 'Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Churn_Rate_Pct] ),
         DATEADD ( 'DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER )
     )
 RETURN
 DIVIDE( CurrentChurn - PrevChurn, PrevChurn ) 
QoQ Network Availability Change = 
 VAR CurrentQ = AVERAGE('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Network_Availability_Pct])/100
 VAR PrevQ =
     CALCULATE(
         AVERAGE('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Network_Availability_Pct])/100,
         DATEADD('DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER)
     )
 RETURN
 DIVIDE(CurrentQ - PrevQ, PrevQ) 
Revenue QoQ(%) = 
 VAR PrevQ =
     CALCULATE(
         [Total Revenue],
         DATEADD( 'DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER )
     )
 RETURN
 DIVIDE( [Total Revenue] - PrevQ, PrevQ ) 
Subs QoQ(%) = 
 VAR CurrentQ = SUM('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Subscribers])
 VAR PrevQ =
     CALCULATE(
         SUM('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[Subscribers]),
         DATEADD('DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER)
     )
 RETURN
 DIVIDE(CurrentQ - PrevQ, PrevQ) 
Subscribers YoY % = VAR Curr = [Total Subscribers]
 VAR Prev = CALCULATE([Total Subscribers], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('DateTable'[Date]))
 RETURN IF( ISBLANK(Prev), BLANK(), DIVIDE(Curr - Prev, Prev) ) 
 YoY Growth = 
 VAR CurrentRevenue =
     [Total Revenue]
 
 VAR RevenueLastYear =
     CALCULATE(
         [Total Revenue],
         SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('DateTable'[Date])
     )
 
 VAR RevenuePrevQuarter =
     CALCULATE(
         [Total Revenue],
         DATEADD('DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER)
     )
 
 RETURN
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     NOT ISBLANK(RevenueLastYear),
         DIVIDE(CurrentRevenue - RevenueLastYear, RevenueLastYear),
 
     NOT ISBLANK(RevenuePrevQuarter),
         DIVIDE(CurrentRevenue - RevenuePrevQuarter, RevenuePrevQuarter),
 
     BLANK()
 ) 
ARPU QoQ(%) = 
 VAR CurrentQ = AVERAGE('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[ARPU_QR])
 VAR PrevQ =
     CALCULATE(
         AVERAGE('Vodafone_quatar_Dataset'[ARPU_QR]),
         DATEADD('DateTable'[Date], -1, QUARTER)
     )
 RETURN
 DIVIDE(CurrentQ - PrevQ, PrevQ) 

Also create DAX formula for the arrows too. It is the same step like the above. we create them by using New measures.

In the modeling Ribbon Select New measure and insert the below DAX formulas one by one.
 
Arrow QoQ ARPU Change = 
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     [ARPU QoQ(%)] < 0, UNICHAR(9662),
     [ARPU QoQ(%)] = 0, UNICHAR(9666),
     UNICHAR(9652)
 ) 
Arrow QoQ Churn Change (%) = 
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     [QoQ Churn Change (%)] < 0, UNICHAR(9662),
     [QoQ Churn Change (%)] = 0, UNICHAR(9666),
     UNICHAR(9652)
 ) 
Arrow QoQ Network Availability Change = 
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     [QoQ Network Availability Change] < 0, UNICHAR(9662),
     [QoQ Network Availability Change] = 0, UNICHAR(9666),
     UNICHAR(9652)
 ) 
Arrow QoQ Revenue Change = 
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     [Revenue QoQ(%)] < 0, UNICHAR(9662),
     [Revenue QoQ(%)] = 0, UNICHAR(9666),
     UNICHAR(9652)
 ) 
Arrow Subscribers QoQ Growth = 
 SWITCH(
     TRUE(),
     [Subs QoQ(%)] < 0, UNICHAR(9662),
     [Subs QoQ(%)] = 0, UNICHAR(9666),
     UNICHAR(9652)
 ) 

Insight: 

How to use Power BI DAX

Step Name:Add Background Image
Detailed Instructions:
Go to Visualization and click the format page
expand canvas background
In the Image part browse background image from the device
Choose the image and click open
Image fit  => Stretch, Transparency => 50%

Insight: 

Take Power BI background to the next level

Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
What if telecom leaders could predict revenue shifts before they happen? This project turns raw Vodafone telecom data into actionable business intelligence. By transforming and enriching the dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric, it uncovers hidden patterns in revenue, churn, and customer behavior. The result is a unified, ML-ready data foundation that enables smarter forecasting, sharper performance tracking, and confident, data-driven decision-making for finance and strategy teams.
Step Name:Adding Logos and Title
Detailed Instructions:

Insert Vodafone LOGO

Go to the Insert click on Shapes
Select Rectangle 
Go to Format Shape and change the following properties:
Shape
       Style -> Fill -> color = #FFFFFF, Border = OFF
General
        Properties -> Size -> Height = 64, Width = 1278, 
                          -> Position -> Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 0
   4. Go to Insert and Click Image
   5. In the Format Image set the following properties:
Image
        Style -> Image -> Image(Browse) -> choose the Vodafone Logo -> Open
                                 -> Image Fit = Fill
General
        Properties -> Size -> Height = 63, Width = 242, 
                          -> Position -> Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 0 

Insert Colaberry LOGO and Title
Go to Insert and Click Image 
In the Format Image set the following properties: 
Image
       Style -> Image -> Image(Browse) -> choose the Colaberry Logo -> Open
                                 -> Image Fit = Fit
General
        Properties -> Size -> Height = 45, Width = 171,
                          -> Position -> Horizontal = 1085, Vertical = 11
   3. Go to Insert and click Text box
   4. Write the title inside the text box, set the Font style = Segoe (Bold),             Size = 20, set the Alignment to center, Font color = #696969

       In the Format text box set the following properties :
General 
        Properties -> Size -> Height = 54, Width = 825,
                          -> Position -> Horizontal = 254, Vertical = 10

                          -> Position -> Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 64

                                     -> Visual boarder = OFF

Add the Line and Clustered column chart in to the Canvas
For the X-axis field bring the YearQuarter from DateTable 
For Column Y-axis bring Total Revenue from Measures Table
For Line Y-axis bring Revenue QoQ(%), Subs QoQ(%), ARPU QoQ(%)
Go to Format Visual and Set the Properties :
Visual
      X-axis -> Values-> Font = Segoe UI (Bold), Size =9, Color = #000000, Title = OFF

General 
        Properties -> Size -> Height = 327, Width = 518,

                          -> Padding = upper side (0)
        Title -> Text =Revenue Trend, Heading = Heading 3, Font = Segeo UI Bold, size = 14, Text color = #FFFFFF, Background color = #B3B3B3, Horizontal Alignment = center
         Effects -> Background -> color = #FFFFFF, Transparency = 35% 
 4. Now we are going to Sort the chart based on the YearQuarter so On the chart Click on the 3 dots 
 5. Click Sort axis -> YearQuarter in ascending order.

portfolioContent.title: Revenue Forecasting and Performance Analysis of Vodafone Qatar (2021–2023)
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a unified ML-ready platform to forecast revenue and analyze performance for Vodafone Qatar’s telecom business from 2021 to 2023.
portfolioContent.summary: This project transformed Vodafone Qatar’s raw telecom data into a consolidated, machine-learning-ready dataset using NexusMax, Python, and Microsoft Fabric. It enabled advanced revenue forecasting, churn analysis, and performance tracking through interactive dashboards, supporting data-driven decisions for finance and strategy teams. The solution uncovered key patterns in customer behavior and network performance to optimize budget planning and reduce revenue risk.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Vodafone Qatar faced challenges in understanding regional and segment revenue performance due to fragmented data and fluctuating subscriber churn. This lack of a unified view hindered accurate forecasting and confident decision-making for finance and strategy teams. The project addressed the need for consolidated insights to improve revenue predictability and operational planning.
objectives: Create a unified, clean, and ML-ready telecom dataset for analysis
Improve quarterly revenue forecasting accuracy to optimize investments
Reduce churn in key customer segments to protect recurring revenue
workflow: Ingest and clean raw Vodafone Qatar telecom data
Engineer and validate key performance metrics and time tables
Develop supervised regression models for revenue forecasting
Build interactive Power BI dashboards for performance tracking
Deploy unified ML-ready dataset for strategic decision-making
keyInsights: Total revenue increased 14% quarter-over-quarter, driven mainly by Doha region
Churn rate for high-value prepaid customers rose by 0.3% QoQ, impacting revenue stability
Network availability remained high at 99.95%, supporting customer retention efforts
Churn in Mobile Postpaid and Fixed Broadband segments exceeded overall churn, risking $8–12K annual revenue loss
businessImpact: Enhanced forecasting accuracy enabled optimized budget allocation across growth segments
Reduced uncertainty in revenue planning through consolidated, real-time insights
Accelerated finance and strategy teams’ access to actionable data, improving decision confidence
